design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through prison colony levels by completing objectives (sealing doors, rescuing prisoners) while managing time limits and combat against aliens and facehuggers
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with enemy encounters; player must navigate maze-like levels, find objectives via computer terminals, and escape before time expires
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Ellen Ripley defending a prison colony from alien infestation using an arsenal of weapons (pulse rifles, flamethrowers, grenades) while solving environmental puzzles
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces strict time limits on each level and requires consulting terminal-based objective selection and layout blueprints, making navigation and planning core to progression rather than pure combat skill

difficulty.lives_system: Player loses a life if level time limit expires; multiple lives system present
difficulty.checkpointing: SNES version includes password feature for level progression; Game Boy version lacks this convenience
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency; time limits are mandatory per level, objective selection is presented but constraints are fixed

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon variety system - player acquires different weapons (pulse rifles, flamethrowers, grenades) throughout progression; motion tracker aids enemy detection
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through sequential levels; objectives unlocked via computer terminals scattered throughout each level
replayability_hooks: Multiple mission objectives per level (sealing doors, rescuing prisoners) create varying routes
Password system on SNES version enables selective replay
Master System version includes two-player option for replayability

world_building_techniques: Prison colony setting from Alien 3 film directly adapted
Computer terminals provide contextual layout blueprints and mission briefs
Cinema scenes in Game Boy version enhance narrative framing

boss_design.philosophy: Single climactic encounter structure; game concludes with Alien Queen battle as final challenge
notable_bosses: Alien Queen - final boss battle at game conclusion

danger_signals: Motion tracker alerts of nearby enemies
Time limit pressure as resource scarcity mechanic
environmental_storytelling: Prison colony architecture and layout (accessible via terminal blueprints)
Elevator systems and ladder networks suggest facility design priorities
Tunnels and crawlspaces for tactical movement
